summary: Hardcoded Stripe API key committed in checkout service
description: Our secret-scanning pipeline flagged a live-looking Stripe API key hardcoded directly in the payment charge path. This key is also being written to application logs on every charge, which multiplies the exposure. Needs to be pulled out into an environment variable / secrets manager immediately and the log line scrubbed.

comments.author: security-bot
comments.body: Auto-filed from secret-scanner run #4821. Match found in paymentService.js.

comments.author: priya.eng
comments.body: Confirmed, this looks like a placeholder/test key but we should treat it as live until proven otherwise. Rotating just in case.

comments.author: priya.eng
comments.body: Assigning to the on-call rotation for an emergency patch.

logs.stacktrace:
at chargeCustomer (watched-repo/paymentService.js:3:9)
at processPayment (watched-repo/checkoutController.js:22:14)
at Router.handle (express/lib/router/index.js:284:12)

summary: Remote code execution risk via eval() in template renderer
description: The template renderer evaluates user-supplied expressions directly with eval(), which means any user who can influence the expression string can run arbitrary JS on the server. A pen-test finding from last week's engagement calls this out as a P1. Needs to be replaced with a safe expression evaluator (no eval/new Function).

comments.author: pentest-report-import
comments.body: Imported from external pentest report, finding #7: 'Server-side template injection via eval()'.

comments.author: arjun.eng
comments.body: Reproduced locally — passing `require('child_process').execSync('id')` as the expression runs it server-side. Definitely needs a fix.

logs.raw:
2026-07-13T09:01:15.002Z [ERROR] Unexpected eval() execution triggered by pentest payload id=finding-7
2026-07-13T09:01:15.010Z [ERROR] child_process spawned from within renderTemplate() — treat as RCE

summary: SQL injection in user search endpoint
description: The user search endpoint builds its SQL query via raw string concatenation of the `name` query param. A crafted name value can break out of the query and inject arbitrary SQL. Needs to move to parameterized queries.

comments.author: arjun.eng
comments.body: Found while reviewing slow-query logs — a request with name=' OR '1'='1 returned the entire users table.

comments.author: priya.eng
comments.body: Confirmed in staging. Marking High since it's an unauthenticated endpoint.

logs.raw: 2026-07-14T09:58:03.221Z [WARN] slow query returned 48213 rows (expected < 50): SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = '' OR '1'='1'
